notes: kiro-cli has no auto-classifier; it prompts for EVERY write/shell by default (read-only auto-runs). This cell exercises the RISKY arc the scenario asserts — a mutating tool call that raises kiro's 'write requires approval' picker (Yes / Trust always in session / No; FINDINGS.md §6). The recipe launches plain `kiro-cli chat` (NO --trust-all-tools), asks kiro to write a file (so the picker appears), drives the picker via `keys` to select 'Yes', and the turn completes. The spec asserts the CORRECT arc ready→working→waiting→working→ready. KNOWN FAILING on the load-bearing waiting observable: while the picker is pending the kiro JSONL already holds the AssistantMessage with the open `write` toolUse and NO further events until approval (FINDINGS.md §2/§5), but the daemon classifies an open tool with no result as working (state_classifier.go rule 4). The only fallback to waiting for a held edit-permission is OpenToolStalled (rule 1b), gated by isPermissionGatedEditTool which matches the exact names Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit (session.go:268) — kiro's lowercase `write` does NOT match, so the pending-approval window never reaches waiting. The local trace plainly carries the open write call, so this is a parser/allowlist bug (extend the permission-gated edit-tool set to kiro's lowercase names), not architectural incapability; kiro has no permission hook (PermissionPending is claudecode-only, rule 0 cannot fire). record files the issue and records known_failing; the spec asserts the correct behavior unweakened. The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar-only, not read by the v1 parser; FINDINGS.md §4), so the model field is EMPTY (omitted from observations, matching the recorded 2-x kiro cells) and cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false. Tracked as ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht#588.

text: kiro-cli chat session appears in ready
User prompt asking kiro to write a file drives ready → working

The daemon currently stays working because kiro's lowercase `write` is not in the permission-gated edit-tool allowlist (isPermissionGatedEditTool, session.go:268) and kiro emits no permission hook — so OpenToolStalled never sets and rule 0 cannot fire. Spec asserts the CORRECT waiting state.
User answers the picker ('Yes') → the write tool executes and emits its toolResult → waiting → working
Turn completes back to ready (text-only AssistantMessage); final state is ready
